---
title: "Multi-Model Reliability Results"
description: "Execution status and field-level diagnostics for blind multi-model annotation stress tests"
---

::: {.callout-important}
These are model-behavior diagnostics, not human inter-annotator reliability or
scholarly evidence. Consensus is not adjudication, and no result proves
reproducibility or changes an accepted annotation.
:::

This page is generated from validated case-local model-reliability artifacts by
`scripts/model_reliability/generate_results_page.py`.

**Layer-only execution:** `lincoln` executed `cmt`, `interpretation` only; `identification` remains designed but not executed.

## Execution status by case and layer

| Case | Task layer | State | Valid runs | Available fields | Note |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| `am-rev` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `am-rev` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `am-rev` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `fr-rev` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `fr-rev` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `fr-rev` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `hitler` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `lincoln` | identification | designed — not executed | 0 | 0 | No valid submissions for this layer; case completion reflects the executed layer(s). |
| `lincoln` | cmt | complete | 2 | 8 | /Users/andrewhammer/codegarden/koenigsberg-sacrifice-workbench/cases/lincoln/corpus/annotated/lincoln-gettysburg-address_annotated.json: packet source input hash predates annotation-forward corpus refresh; existing packet remains historical and requires reliability resampling before promotion. |
| `lincoln` | interpretation | complete | 2 | 17 | /Users/andrewhammer/codegarden/koenigsberg-sacrifice-workbench/cases/lincoln/corpus/annotated/lincoln-gettysburg-address_annotated.json: packet source input hash predates annotation-forward corpus refresh; existing packet remains historical and requires reliability resampling before promotion. |
| `napoleon` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `napoleon` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `napoleon` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `wwi-britain` | identification | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `wwi-britain` | cmt | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |
| `wwi-britain` | interpretation | absent — not designed | 0 | 0 | Multi-model reliability has not been designed for this case. |

Layer state is computed from validated submissions for that layer. `Complete`
means the artifact chain for the executed layer is valid; it does not mean an
interpretation has been proven.

## Model-to-model field stability

| Case | Language | Layer | Field | Run pair | N | Metric | Value | Cohen's kappa |
|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| `lincoln` | en | cmt | `cmt.cluster_id` | `issue147-anthropic-cmt-final2-20260627` ↔ `issue147-openai-cmt-final2-20260627` | 7 | observed_agreement | 1 | undefined: not applicable to this field type |

| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `uncertainty.status` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` ↔ `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` | 7 | observed_agreement | 0.571 | 0.222 |

Model-to-model stability asks whether separately run systems behave similarly.
Shared behavior may reflect a clear instruction, shared bias, prompt effects,
or common training data.

## Model-to-reference field divergence

| Case | Language | Layer | Field | Comparison | N | Metric | Value |

| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.purification` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.purification` | `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacred_object` | `issue147-anthropic-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacred_object` | `issue147-openai-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacred_object` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacred_object` | `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacrificial_body` | `issue147-anthropic-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacrificial_body` | `issue147-openai-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacrificial_body` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.sacrificial_body` | `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.violence_logic` | `issue147-anthropic-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.violence_logic` | `issue147-openai-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.violence_logic` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `interpretation.functions.violence_logic` | `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `uncertainty.status` | `issue147-anthropic-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `uncertainty.status` | `issue147-openai-cmt-final2-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `uncertainty.status` | `issue157-anthropic-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |
| `lincoln` | en | interpretation | `uncertainty.status` | `issue157-openai-interpretation-20260627` vs `lincoln:accepted-reference` | 0 | observed_agreement | undefined: no comparable observations |

Reference alignment is reported separately from model-to-model stability. A
divergence may reflect model error, reference error, or codebook ambiguity; it
is a question for human review, not an automatic correction.

## Consensus, instability, and review queue

| Case | Fields | Stable | Unstable | Insufficient data | Review items | High priority |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `lincoln` | 34 | 2 | 23 | 9 | 160 | 125 |

The review queue has human-only decision authority. Model agreement is not a
vote, and high-priority reference challenges remain unadjudicated suggestions.

## Interpretation limits

- Results remain stratified by case, source language, task layer, field, and
  run or run pair.
- Undefined and unavailable metrics stay visible rather than becoming zero or
  perfect agreement.
- Missing submissions and reports are execution states, not positive findings.
- Model outputs cannot corroborate historical claims or validate theoretical
  interpretations.
- Human source review, adjudication, and accepted-correction governance remain
  separate from this stress test.

See the [methodology](model-reliability-methodology.qmd) for the study design and
the [external review procedure](docs/reliability/external-model-review-procedures.md)
for packet execution and ingestion.
